name: Holly Kim
bio: Holly Kim is the Lake County Treasurer, currently serving her second term, and is running for Illinois Comptroller in the four-way Democratic primary on March 17, 2026. She earned a high school diploma from Glenbrook North High School and a bachelor's degree from Northeastern Illinois University. If elected, she would make history as the first Asian American elected to a statewide constitutional office in Illinois. Kim was first elected Lake County Treasurer in 2018 and reelected. As Lake County Treasurer, she oversees an office that processes and cuts $73.5 million in checks and $2.8 billion in ACH distributions annually — a function she argues is directly analogous to the Comptroller's core work. Under her leadership, the Lake County Treasurer's office secured record investment earnings of over $3.2 million in a single year, representing a 200%+ increase from when she took office. She serves as a Commissioner for the Bank On Illinois initiative under the Illinois Comptroller's Office, giving her direct familiarity with the statewide operation. She is affiliated with Mano a Mano, the Hanul Family Alliance, the Illinois Asian American Caucus, the Democratic Party of Lake County, and the Fremont Township Democrats. The four-candidate Democratic primary also includes Rep. Margaret Croke (top fundraiser), Rep. Karina Villa, and Rep. Stephanie Kifowit. Kim carries the explicit endorsement of outgoing Comptroller Susana Mendoza. Kim has described her background in technology and process improvement as core to her platform, including pushing back against misuse of license plate reader data as a Village Trustee in Mundelein in 2013.
prior_office: Lake County Treasurer (2018–present, serving second term); Village Trustee, Mundelein (prior); Township Director, Fremont Township

jacket.note: ILSBE committee ID 32198 ('Friends of Holly Kim') confirmed. Dec 31, 2025 quarterly report: $240,991.30 funds available + $86,945.20 contributions since Dec 31 = $327,936.50 cash on hand. $34.59 in investments, $2,000 in debts. Top donor: Teamsters Local 705 PAC Account $78,000 (March 11, 2026 — very recent). Holly Kim herself contributed $23,362.60. Lake County Democratic Central Committee gave $21,322.77. Ethereum donation of $8,158.84 (cryptocurrency contribution — notable). Old National Bank, Clarity Partners LLC, Leonard Law Group, and multiple Teamster locals all in top donors, indicating strong labor and local business support base. Fourth in fundraising in IL Comptroller race.

donors.name: Tracy Katz Muhl for State Central Committeeperson
Teamsters Local Union No. 731 PAC Fund
Teamsters Local Union No. 727 Political Action Committee

jacket.donors_note: Itemized donor data not available from public ISBE or OpenSecrets as of data date. Marked limited.

key_votes.bill: License Plate Reader (LPR) Data Privacy Restrictions — Mundelein Village Board, 2013
key_votes.summary: As Village Trustee in Mundelein, Kim pushed back against proposed uses of license plate reader data, insisting on strong guardrails to prevent surveillance overreach. The policy was adopted with her restrictions in place. Kim cites this as foundational to her ongoing data privacy advocacy.

endorsements: Liberal Moms of the NW Suburbs

career_history.highlight: Serving second term. Oversees $2.8B in ACH distributions annually. Achieved record investment earnings of over $3.2M in a single year (200%+ increase). Manages unionized AFSCME workforce. Added credit unions as property tax collection sites to improve access for working families.
Pushed back against misuse of LPR data. Only board member to oppose Governor Rauner's Turnaround Agenda; her pushback helped prevent it from advancing.
Local government executive experience before county-level role.
Current service under the office she seeks, giving her direct operational familiarity.

career_history.org: Office of IL Secretary of State-elect Alexi Giannoulias
career_history.highlight: Provided technology modernization recommendations; proposed leveraging public libraries as extended customer service options for government technology.

policy_platform.position: Will use Comptroller's office as a firewall against improper data sharing, block outside access to state financial and tax records, and protect Illinoisans' personal information from political weaponization.

Strengthen and expand enforcement of prevailing wage laws; invest in the Comptroller's Prevailing Wage Department to ensure workers on public projects are paid legally required wages. Pro-union — manages AFSCME-represented workforce as Treasurer.
Phase out paper checks to prevent check-washing fraud, implement secure digital tools like DocuSign, upgrade cybersecurity, standardize payment documentation for clearer tracking of public dollars.
Expand public dashboards on state spending, enforce municipal financial reporting requirements, ensure timely payments to social service providers and local governments.
Supports a progressive state income tax as part of improving Illinois' credit rating. Plans to leverage Comptroller's platform to advocate for long-term fiscal stability.

social_pulse.summary: Strong engagement in suburban progressive and Asian American communities. Well-organized endorsement network. Lower media profile than Croke or Kifowit at the state level, but strong grassroots base in Lake County and northern Chicago suburbs. Her Mendoza endorsement is the most significant institutional backing in the comptroller race.

trust_indicators.label: Endorsed by incumbent Comptroller Susana Mendoza — confirmed
Two terms as elected Lake County Treasurer — confirmed
Manages $2.8B in ACH distributions annually — confirmed via Ballotpedia survey
No ethics violations or criminal matters in public record
Campaign finance totals not publicly available as of March 14, 2026

news_hits.headline: Primary election 2026: Illinois comptroller candidates Margaret Croke, Karina Villa, Holly Kim, Stephanie Kifowit appeal to voters
news_hits.title: Primary election 2026: Illinois comptroller candidates Margaret Croke, Karina Villa, Holly Kim, Stephanie Kifowit appeal to voters
news_hits.summary: ABC7 Chicago (Mar 6) profiles all four Democratic candidates for Illinois Comptroller ahead of the March 17 primary. Holly Kim, Lake County Treasurer, highlights her fiscal management background and anti-predatory lending work as key qualifications.

news_hits.headline: What to know about the March 17 primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.title: What to know about the March 17 primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: WBEZ (Feb 17) explains the four-way Democratic primary for Illinois Comptroller to replace retiring Susana Mendoza. Holly Kim (Lake County Treasurer) is running alongside Croke, Villa, and Kifowit. The piece highlights Kim's suburban fiscal management background.

news_hits.title: Susana Mendoza endorses Holly Kim to replace her as Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: Outgoing Illinois Comptroller Susana Mendoza endorsed Holly Kim to be her successor, choosing the Lake County Treasurer over three Democratic rivals vying for the March 17 primary nomination.

news_hits.title: Four Democrats battle in March primary to succeed Susana Mendoza as Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: Chicago Sun-Times race preview of four Democratic candidates for Illinois comptroller: Lake County Treasurer Holly Kim, state Sen. Karina Villa, state Rep. Stephanie Kifowit, and state Rep. Margaret Croke. Kim is described as the best-funded candidate backed by major party endorsements.

news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ comprehensive March 13 primary preview covers all major races including the Illinois comptroller contest featuring Holly Kim, Karina Villa, Stephanie Kifowit, and Margaret Croke as the four-way Democratic primary.

news_hits.title: Column: Ballot features unusual number of open federal seats
news_hits.summary: Tribune column notes the rare opening for Illinois Comptroller where four Democrats compete, including Lake County Treasurer Holly Kim of Mundelein. Highlights Kim as one of four candidates replacing non-incumbent Susana Mendoza.

news_hits.title: Illinois primary: What to know for Tuesday's election
news_hits.summary: Tribune day-before guide includes the Illinois comptroller race, showing Kim alongside Croke, Villa, and Kifowit in a four-way Democratic primary debate at Des Plaines Public Library on Feb. 6, 2026.

news_hits.title: Turnout of 15% expected for Tuesday's Lake County primaries
news_hits.summary: Tribune election-eve report on Lake County primary turnout expectations. Lake County Clerk Anthony Vega projects ~15% of the county's 461,338 registered voters will cast ballots. Relevant to Holly Kim — the Lake County Treasurer — as she depends heavily on Lake County name recognition and local Democratic support as her base for the statewide Comptroller race.

news_hits.title: Illinois comptroller candidates 2026: Who's replacing Susana Mendoza?
news_hits.summary: Holly Kim, endorsed by outgoing Comptroller Susana Mendoza, currently serves as Lake County Treasurer and is running for IL Comptroller in the Democratic primary.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois primary day election results
news_hits.summary: Illinois primary day featuring competitive races including the IL Comptroller Democratic primary where Holly Kim faces challengers.

news_hits.title: State Rep. Margaret Croke declares victory in Democratic primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: State Rep. Margaret Croke declared victory in the Illinois Comptroller Democratic primary, defeating Holly Kim (Lake County Treasurer), Karina Villa, and Stephanie Kifowit. Outgoing Comptroller Susana Mendoza had endorsed Holly Kim as her replacement, but Croke's Chicago base and campaign support carried the race.

news_hits.title: Illinois comptroller race: Democratic State Rep. Croke has narrow lead
news_hits.summary: Tribune reports Croke declared victory in a tightly contested Illinois Comptroller race. Holly Kim, endorsed by outgoing Comptroller Mendoza and backed by significant labor support, placed in a multi-candidate field behind Croke.

news_hits.title: Holly Kim finishes third in Illinois Comptroller Democratic primary
news_hits.summary: Peoria Journal Star reports final primary results showing State Rep. Margaret Croke winning the Illinois Comptroller Democratic primary. Lake County Treasurer Holly Kim finished third with approximately 22.4% of the vote, behind Croke (37.1%) and state Sen. Karina Villa (33.1%).

news_hits.title: 'This election is not over': Democratic race for Illinois comptroller still undecided as Croke holds slim lead
news_hits.summary: Tribune morning-after report confirms the Illinois Comptroller Democratic primary remains too close to call. Holly Kim, who had Comptroller Susana Mendoza's endorsement, finished a distant third with ~24% of votes counted at 92% reporting, trailing leader Croke (34.6%) and Villa (32.2%). Approximately 88,000 Chicago mail-in ballots remain outstanding. Kim's third-place finish is a significant setback for her campaign.

news_hits.title: Margaret Croke wins Democratic primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: NPR Illinois confirms state Rep. Margaret Croke won the Democratic primary for Illinois Comptroller, defeating Holly Kim, Karina Villa, and Stephanie Kifowit. Croke received the most votes; Kifowit came in last with 8.7%. The AP called the race on March 19 after outstanding Chicago mail-in ballots were largely counted. Croke's victory was described as another win for Gov. Pritzker, who had backed her. Kim, the Lake County Treasurer, received approximately 24% in the final tally.

news_hits.title: Super PAC scorecard — how outside spending groups fared in efforts to influence Illinois primary voters
news_hits.summary: WBEZ post-primary spending analysis. Holly Kim's Comptroller primary loss came despite competitive fundraising; the race was dominated by Croke's better-organized Chicago-area network and Pritzker's backing. Kim was the only suburban candidate in a field that skewed toward Chicago-based candidates with stronger county organization.

news_hits.title: Illinois 2026 primary election results: Margaret Croke wins Democratic primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: Croke wins Democratic comptroller primary, defeating Holly Kim and others. Croke received 39% of Cook County vote; Kim, endorsed by outgoing Comptroller Susana Mendoza, finished third.

news_hits.title: Margaret Croke wins Democratic primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: Official confirmation that Holly Kim lost the Democratic primary to Margaret Croke, who secured the nomination over Villa and Kim. Croke led in Cook County while Villa performed best in west suburban counties.

news_hits.title: Illinois Comptroller Primary 2026: Live Election Results
news_hits.summary: NYT live results tracker for the Illinois Comptroller Democratic primary shows Holly Kim finished third with approximately 24.4% of the statewide vote, behind winner Margaret Croke and Karina Villa. Kim dominated her home Lake County, winning more than half of primary voters there, but could not overcome the Chicago-area advantages of the other candidates.

news_hits.title: State Rep. Margaret Croke wins Democratic primary for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times confirms final certified results: Margaret Croke defeated Holly Kim, Karina Villa, and Stephanie Kifowit in the Democratic primary for Illinois Comptroller. Outgoing Comptroller Susana Mendoza had endorsed Kim as her preferred successor. Kim finished third or fourth in the four-way primary race, ending her bid to replace Mendoza.

news_hits.title: North Side lawmaker Margaret Croke secures Democratic nomination for Illinois comptroller
news_hits.summary: Tribune reports Croke officially secured the Democratic comptroller nomination. Lake County Treasurer Holly Kim, who had Comptroller Mendoza's endorsement, finished third with 24.4% of the vote — a significant setback for Kim despite high-profile backing. Kim conceded late Tuesday night.

news_hits.title: Illinois Democrats ran on anti-Trump messaging — Kim's comptroller loss analyzed as funding disadvantage
news_hits.summary: Post-primary Sun-Times analysis by Lynn Sweet frames the comptroller primary as a race where Kim — endorsed by outgoing Comptroller Susana Mendoza — was outspent by both Croke ($1.8M, backed by Pritzker and Michael Sacks) and Villa ($1.4M, backed by CTU). Kim received 24.4% of the vote, finishing third. The broader piece frames Illinois Democratic primaries as nationalized, cash-dominated contests where established endorsements (like Mendoza's for Kim) carried less weight than billionaire and institutional money.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, La Shawn Ford and other Illinois primary winners who beat deeper pockets
news_hits.summary: Rich Miller's post-primary column on winners who outspent expectations includes analysis of the comptroller primary. Holly Kim, the incumbent, lost to State Rep. Margaret Croke despite Kim's incumbency advantage, as the Cook County Democratic Party slate backed Croke and Pritzker-aligned forces mobilized against Kim. The column frames Kim's loss as part of a broader pattern of machine-organized Democrats displacing reformers across the March 17 ballot.

news_hits.title: Illinois comptroller general election set: Croke vs. Republican challenger
news_hits.summary: Post-primary Tribune analysis of Illinois outside spending confirms Holly Kim's loss to Margaret Croke in the comptroller primary. Kim was outspent and failed to secure key party organization backing. Croke now faces the Republican nominee in the November general election. Kim's path to any future office will require rebuilding name recognition and donor base after being unseated from the comptroller's office.

news_hits.title: State lawmakers advance bill to keep ICE detention centers out of communities
news_hits.summary: An Illinois House committee advanced legislation barring new ICE detention facilities from residential neighborhoods. As Cook County Clerk-elect (won uncontested Democratic primary), Holly Kim oversees the election infrastructure that processed March 17 primary results. The legislation is the dominant Cook County post-primary policy story and directly involves her county constituency.

news_hits.title: Chicago young voter turnout increases in 2026 midterm primary
news_hits.summary: About 85,000 Chicago voters ages 17–34 voted in Tuesday's midterm primary — up from 62,000 in the 2022 primary, a 37% increase. As incoming Cook County Clerk responsible for administering elections, Holly Kim inherits an energized electorate with high youth participation. The trend is a positive signal for her general election prospects and for her office's outreach goals.

primary_result.pct_note: Early count showed 20.74%; Tribune March 18 update shows ~24% at 92% reporting — final count pending outstanding Chicago mail-in ballots
